segments.text: JACK: I've still got loads to do for our report on nutritional food labels.
segments.translation: 杰克：我们关于营养食品标签的报告还有很多事要做。

segments.text: ALICE: Me too. What did you learn from doing the project about your own shopping habits?
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：我也是。你从你购物习惯的课题学到了什么呢？

segments.text: JACK: Well, I've always had to check labels for traces of peanuts in everything I eat because of my allergy.
segments.translation: 杰克：恩，因为我的过敏症，所以一直要检查我吃的每个东西的标签查看食品是否含有花生。

segments.text: But beyond that I've never really been concerned enough to check how healthy a product is.
segments.translation: 但除此之外，我从未真正关心过一个产品有多健康。

segments.text: ALICE: This project has actually taught me to read the labels much more carefully.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：这个课题实际上教会了我更仔细地阅读标签。

segments.text: I tended to believe claims on packaging like 'low in fat'.
segments.translation: 我以前倾向于相信包装，比如"脂肪很低"。

segments.text: But I now realise that the 'healthy' yoghurt I've bought for years is full of sugar and that it's actually quite high in calories.
segments.translation: 但我现在意识到，我多年来买的"健康"酸奶含有大量的糖分，而且实际上卡路里含量很高。

segments.text: JACK: Ready meals are the worst ... comparing the labels on supermarket pizzas was a real eye-opener.
segments.translation: 杰克：熟食最糟糕……比较超市比萨饼上的标签真是令人大开眼界。

segments.text: Did you have any idea how many calories they contain? I was amazed.
segments.translation: 你知道它们含有多少卡路里吗？我真的很震惊。

segments.text: ALICE: Yes, because unless you read the label really carefully, you wouldn't know that the nutritional values given are for half a pizza.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：是的，因为除非你仔细阅读标签，你不会知道给你的营养价值是半个比萨饼。

segments.text: JACK: When most people eat the whole pizza.
segments.translation: 杰克：大多数人都会吃掉整个披萨。

segments.text: Not exactly transparent is it?
segments.translation: 说得很模糊，不是吗？

segments.text: ALICE: Not at all. But I expect it won't stop you from buying pizza?
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：是的。但我想这并不会阻止你继续购买披萨吧？

segments.text: JACK: Probably not, no!
segments.translation: 杰克：当然不会啦，绝不！

segments.text: I thought comparing the different labelling systems used by food manufacturers was interesting.
segments.translation: 我觉得比较食物生产商使用的不同标签系统非常有趣。

segments.text: I think the kind of labelling system used makes a big difference.
segments.translation: 我觉得使用的标签系统的类型有很大不同。

segments.text: ALICE: Which one did you prefer?
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：你喜欢哪一个呢？

segments.text: JACK: I liked the traditional daily value system best - the one which tells you what proportion of your required daily intake of each ingredient the product contains.
segments.translation: 杰克：我最喜欢传统的推荐每日摄取量体系。它告诉你产品中每种成分在你每日所需摄入量中所占的比例。

segments.text: I'm not sure it's the easiest for people to use but at least you get the full story.
segments.translation: 我并不确定这是最方便人们使用的，但是至少你会知道所有的东西。

segments.text: I like to know all the ingredients in a product - not just how much fat, salt and sugar they contain.
segments.translation: 我想知道一种产品的所有成分——不仅仅是它们含有多少脂肪、盐和糖。

segments.text: ALICE: But it's good supermarkets have been making an effort to provide reliable information for customers.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：但是好的超市一直在努力为顾客提供可靠的信息。

segments.text: JACK: Yes. There just needs to be more consistency between labelling systems used by different supermarkets, in terms of portion sizes, etc.
segments.translation: 杰克：是的。不同超市使用的标签系统之间需要有更多的一致性，比如在份量等方面。

segments.text: ALICE: Mmm. The labels on the different brands of chicken flavour crisps were quite revealing too, weren't they?
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：嗯。不同牌子的鸡肉味薯片上的标签也很能说明问题，不是吗？

segments.text: JACK: Yeah. I don't understand how they can get away with calling them chicken flavour when they only contain artificial additives.
segments.translation: 杰克：是的。我不明白，当它们只含有人工添加剂时，它们怎么能被称为鸡肉味呢？

segments.text: ALICE: I know. I'd at least have expected them to contain a small percentage of real chicken.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：我知道。至少我以为里面会含有一点点真的鸡肉。

segments.text: JACK: Absolutely.
segments.translation: 杰克：是的。

segments.text: ALICE: I think having nutritional food labeling has been a good idea, don't you?
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：我认为贴营养食品标签是个好主意，你说呢?

segments.text: I think it will change people's behaviour and stop mothers, in particular, buying the wrong things.
segments.translation: 我认为它将改变人们的行为，特别是阻止妈妈们买错误的东西。

segments.text: JACK: But didn't that study kind of prove the opposite?
segments.translation: 杰克：但是这个研究不是证明了相反的结果吗？

segments.text: People didn't necessarily stop buying unhealthy products.
segments.translation: 人们没有必要停止买不健康的食物。

segments.text: ALICE: They only said that might be the case.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：他们只是说可能会那样。

segments.text: Those findings weren't that conclusive and it was quite a small-scale study.
segments.translation: 这些发现并不是结论性的，这是一个小范围的研究。

segments.text: I think more research has to be done.
segments.translation: 我想还需要做更多的研究。

segments.text: JACK: Yes, I think you're probably right.
segments.translation: 杰克：是的，我觉得你可能是对的。

segments.text: JACK: What do you think of the traffic-light system?
segments.translation: 杰克：你觉得交通信号灯系统怎么样？

segments.text: ALICE: I think supermarkets like the idea of having a colour-coded system - red, orange or green - for levels of fat, sugar and salt in a product.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：我认为超市喜欢用颜色编码系统--红色、橙色或绿色--来表示产品中脂肪、糖和盐的含量。

segments.text: JACK: But it's not been adopted universally.
segments.translation: 杰克：但它并没有被普遍采用。

segments.text: And not on all products.
segments.translation: 并不是所有的产品都使用了。

segments.text: Why do you suppose that is?
segments.translation: 你觉得为什么会那样呢？

segments.text: ALICE: Pressure from the food manufacturers.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：来自于食物生产商的压力。

segments.text: Hardly surprising that some of them are opposed to flagging up how unhealthy their products are.
segments.translation: 毫不奇怪，他们中的一些人反对让大家关注他们的产品有多不健康。

segments.text: JACK: I'd have thought it would have been compulsory.
segments.translation: 杰克：我本来以为这是强制性的

segments.text: It seems ridiculous it isn't.
segments.translation: 不是强制性的看起来很荒谬。

segments.text: ALICE: I know. And what I couldn't get over is the fact that it was brought in without enough consultation - a lot of experts had deep reservations about it.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：我知道。我不能理解的是没有经过足够的商讨，它就被引入使用了。很多专家对此持保留意见。

segments.text: JACK: That is a bit weird.
segments.translation: 杰克：那是有点奇怪。

segments.text: I suppose there's an argument for doing the research now when consumers are familiar with this system.
segments.translation: 我觉得做这个研究可能有点争议，因为消费者已经熟悉了这个系统。

segments.text: ALICE: Yeah, maybe.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：恩，可能是吧。

segments.text: JACK: The participants in the survey were quite positive about the traffic-light system.
segments.translation: 杰克：调查里的参与者对交通信号系统表现得非常积极。

segments.text: ALICE: Mmm. But I don't think they targeted the right people.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：恩。但是我觉得他们没有针对正确的人群。

segments.text: They should have focused on people with low literacy levels because these labels are designed to be accessible to them.
segments.translation: 他们应该侧重于文化水平不高的人，因为这些标签是为了方便他们而设计的。

segments.text: JACK: Yeah. But it's good to get feedback from all socio-economic groups.
segments.translation: 杰克：是的。但是如果能从所有的社会经济群体收集反馈是很好的。

segments.text: And there wasn't much variation in their responses.
segments.translation: 而且他们的回答差别不大。

segments.text: ALICE: No. But if they hadn't interviewed participants face-to-face, they could have used a much bigger sample size.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：是的。但是如果他们没有面对面采访参与者，他们本可以使用更大的样本量。

segments.text: I wonder why they chose that method?
segments.translation: 我在想他们为什么选择那种方法？

segments.text: JACK: Dunno. How were they selected?
segments.translation: 杰克：不知道。参与者是怎么选择的？

segments.text: Did they volunteer or were they approached?
segments.translation: 他们是主动参与还是被动的呢？

segments.text: ALICE: I think they volunteered.
segments.translation: 爱丽丝：我觉得是他们主动的。

segments.text: The thing that wasn't stated was how often they bought packaged food - all we know is how frequently they used the supermarket.
segments.translation: 不是很明白的一点就是他们多久买一次包装食物，我们所知道的是他们多久去一次超市。
